name: create-customers-master-data-collection
description: Create Firestore customers collection with master data fields needed by buying service (credit_limit, default_sales_type). Add to collections.py. Create seed with realistic customer data matching BQ temp_invoice customer_ids.
instruction:
1. Add CUSTOMERS_MASTER = 'customers_master' to my_customers/backend/app/collections.py (not 'customers' to avoid confusion with customer_exclusions concept)
2. Create seed function seed_customers_master() in seed.py with ~30 customers for PL, NL, DE matching customer_ids from BQ mock data (bigquery_stub.py) and mock_data.py
3. Each customer: customer_id (str), customer_name (str), country (str), credit_limit (float, in local currency), default_sales_type (str: Economic|Legal), legal_entity_code (str, FK to OpCo), status (str: active|inactive)
4. Include realistic credit limits: range 10K-500K per customer
5. Call seed_customers_master() in seed.py main seed_all() function
acceptance_criteria: Customer records include credit_limit and default_sales_type fields
customer_ids match those in mock_data.py and bigquery_stub.py

name: create-sales-types-collection
description: Create Firestore sales_types collection with dynamic sales type definitions. Seed with Economic, Legal, Insurance, No.
instruction:
1. Add SALES_TYPES = 'sales_types' to collections.py
2. Create seed function seed_sales_types() with: Economic (eligible for auto-buy), Legal (eligible for auto-buy), Insurance (eligible for manual buy only), No (not eligible, collection pass-through)
3. Each sales_type: code (str), name (str), eligible_auto_buy (bool), eligible_manual_buy (bool), description (str)
4. Call in seed_all()
acceptance_criteria: 4 sales types seeded: Economic, Legal, Insurance, No
Each type has eligibility flags for auto-buy and manual-buy

name: decision-legal-entities-vs-opcos
description: Document decision: legal_entities as separate collection or keep using opcos. Current code uses opco_map keyed by legal_entity_code — creating separate collection would break buying service.
instruction:
Record decision in Forge: legal_entities data is stored on opcos collection (code, name, start_date, country). A separate legal_entities collection is NOT needed because:
1. buying/service.py step_2 queries opco_map by legal_entity_code
2. OpCo code IS the legal entity code in current data model
3. project.md says 'One OpCo can have many Legal Entities' but current BQ data treats them as 1:1
4. Creating separate collection requires refactoring buying service without business value
Record as CLOSED decision.
acceptance_criteria: Reasoning documents why separate collection is not needed

name: verify-o002
description: Deep-verify O-002: check customers_master seed matches BQ mock data, sales_types seeded, ruff passes.
instruction:
1. ruff check on modified files
2. Verify customer_ids in seed match those in bigquery_stub.py and mock_data.py
3. Verify sales_types match what buying service expects
4. Update O-002 KR progress
acceptance_criteria: Customer IDs are consistent across seed, mock data, and BQ stub
